title: Gone Fishin' for Flavor!
story: Every June 25, catfish lovers across America celebrate National Catfish Day by chowing down on this whiskered wonder! Restaurants serve up crispy fried catfish with hush puppies and coleslaw, while families gather for backyard fish fries. Some people even try their hand at catfish noodling - catching these slippery swimmers with their bare hands! Whether you like yours blackened, grilled, or classic Southern-fried, this holiday proves that catfish deserves serious respect on your dinner plate.
activity_question: What do some brave people use to catch catfish during National Catfish Day celebrations?
bonus_fact: Catfish can actually taste with their entire body, not just their mouth!

title: Parfait-ly Sweet Strawberry Day!
story: Every June 25, dessert lovers celebrate National Strawberry Parfait Day by layering sweet strawberries, creamy yogurt, and crunchy granola into tall glasses. Restaurants offer special parfait deals, while families create their own colorful towers at home. Social media fills with photos of these beautiful layered treats that look almost too pretty to eat. Some people add whipped cream, nuts, or even chocolate chips to make their parfaits extra special. It's the perfect summer celebration since strawberries are at their juiciest!
activity_question: What are the main ingredients people layer to make strawberry parfaits?
bonus_fact: The word 'parfait' comes from French and means 'perfect'!

title: Log Cabin Day: Building Memories!
story: Every June 25, people celebrate Log Cabin Day by honoring these amazing wooden homes! Families visit historic log cabins at museums and parks to see how pioneers lived long ago. Some people build mini log cabins from Lincoln Logs or craft sticks. Others share photos of real log cabins they've visited or stayed in during vacations. It's a perfect day to appreciate these sturdy, cozy homes made from trees!
activity_question: What do some people build on Log Cabin Day?
activity_choices: Mini log cabins from Lincoln Logs or craft sticks
Paper airplanes that look like logs
activity_answer: Mini log cabins from Lincoln Logs or craft sticks
follow_up_question: What would be the coolest thing about living in a log cabin?

title: Celebrating Beautiful Differences on World Vitiligo Day
story: People around the world celebrate World Vitiligo Day on June 25 to support those with vitiligo, a condition that creates white patches on skin. Families share photos, wear purple ribbons, and spread messages about how everyone's skin is beautiful just the way it is, no matter what it looks like.
activity_question: What do people wear on World Vitiligo Day to show support?
follow_up_question: How can you help someone feel included if they look different from you?

title: Color TV Day: When Screens Got Rainbow Magic
story: Color TV Day on June 25 celebrates the amazing invention that brought rainbows to our living rooms! Before color television, families gathered around black and white screens that showed everything in shades of gray. Today, people celebrate by watching their favorite colorful shows, sharing throwback photos of old TV sets, and posting about how far technology has come. Some families even have "retro TV nights" where they watch classic shows that first aired in brilliant color. It's wild to think that seeing a red fire truck or blue ocean on TV once seemed like pure magic!
activity_question: What did television screens show before color TV was invented?
bonus_fact: The first color TV show was broadcast in 1951, but most families didn't own color TVs until the 1970s!

title: National Bourdain Day: Adventure on a Plate
story: Food lovers everywhere celebrate National Bourdain Day on June 25 by trying something completely new and delicious! This special day honors Anthony Bourdain, a famous chef and TV host who traveled the world showing people amazing foods from different cultures. Today, people mark this holiday by visiting ethnic restaurants, cooking recipes from other countries, or even just trying that weird-looking dish they've always been curious about. It's all about being brave with your taste buds and discovering flavors from around the globe!
activity_question: How do people typically celebrate National Bourdain Day?
activity_choices: By trying new foods from different cultures
By only eating hamburgers and pizza
By cooking the same meal every day
activity_answer: By trying new foods from different cultures
follow_up_question: What's the most adventurous food you'd be willing to try from another country?

title: Saluting Our Sea Heroes
story: Every June 25, people around the world celebrate Day of the Seafarer to honor the brave sailors who work on ships and boats across our oceans. These hardworking men and women spend months at sea, bringing us everything from toys to food by sailing cargo ships between countries. On this special day, ports fly colorful flags, maritime organizations post thank-you messages on social media, and some cities hold parades near their harbors. It's a chance to remember that almost everything we buy traveled by ship at some point!
activity_question: What do seafarers do that helps bring us everyday items?
activity_choices: They sail cargo ships between countries
They build new boats and ships
They teach people how to swim
activity_answer: They sail cargo ships between countries
follow_up_question: What's something in your room that probably traveled by ship to get to you?
